pull.title: [Feature] Add SM103 (Blackwell Ultra) Support to vLLM
pull.body:
## Summary

This PR introduces initial **SM103 support** needed to run vLLM on NVIDIA’s (G)B300 GPUs. The main focus so far has been validating correct functionality and ensuring that key kernels, quantization paths, and MoE components work reliably on Blackwell Ultra GPUs.

- All tests in `tests/quantization/test_blackwell_moe.py` **pass**.
- Resolved Triton issues related to SM103 support (details below).
- Verified that `nvidia/DeepSeek-V3.1-NVFP4` runs correctly.
    - To fit the model on a single GB300 GPU, vLLM must be launched with: `--cpu-offload-gb 160`
  
### Installation Method

The environment was set up using a pre-installed PyTorch build compiled for CUDA 13.0, followed by:

```bash
python use_existing_torch.py \
  && pip install -r requirements/build.txt \
  && MAX_JOBS=16 pip install --no-build-isolation -e .
```
  
### Triton Fix Required for SM103

The installed Triton version (3.5.1) fails to compile kernels for SM103: 

```
Value 'sm_103a' is not defined for option 'gpu-name'
```

I resolved this by forcing Triton to use the system CUDA ptxas instead of its bundled one:

```bash
export TRITON_PTXAS_PATH=/usr/local/cuda/bin/ptxas
export PATH=/usr/local/cuda/bin:$PATH
export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/usr/local/cuda/lib64:${LD_LIBRARY_PATH:-}
```

This ensures all kernels compile successfully.
  
## Additional Testing

Beyond quantization tests, I also ran several Blackwell-dependent test suites:

#### Passing

```tests/kernels/moe/test_ocp_mx_moe.py```
```tests/compile/distributed/test_fusions_e2e.py```
`tests/kernels/attention/test_cutlass_mla_decode.py`
`tests/kernels/attention/test_flashinfer_trtllm_attention.py`

labels.description: ONLY add when PR is ready to merge/full CI is needed

@@ -301,6 +301,21 @@ def is_device_capability(
 
         return current_capability.to_int() == capability
 
+    @classmethod
+    def is_device_capability_family(
+        cls,
+        capability: int,
+        device_id: int = 0,
+    ) -> bool:
+        """
+        Returns True if the device capability is any <major>.x.
+        Mirrors CUDA 13 'family' architecture semantics (e.g. 10.x, 11.x, 12.x).
+        """
+        current_capability = cls.get_device_capability(device_id=device_id)
+        if current_capability is None:
+            return False
+        return (current_capability.to_int() // 10) == (capability // 10)
+
     @classmethod
     def get_device_name(cls, device_id: int = 0) -> str:
         """Get the name of a device."""
